words.definition: to leave someone or something completely and not return

words.example: As a precaution, they packed a spare torch.

words.definition: from the time before written records
words.example: The cave paintings are prehistoric, older than any city.

words.definition: to keep safe from harm or decay
words.example: The society works to preserve the old windmill.

words.definition: respect and admiration earned by achievements
words.example: Winning the science prize brought the school great prestige.

words.definition: an animal hunted by another for food
words.example: The owl watched its prey from the fence post.

words.definition: to explore or examine closely
words.example: The dentist probed gently for the sore tooth.

words.definition: a line of people or vehicles moving forward ceremonially
words.example: A procession of floats wound through the town.

words.definition: to announce publicly or officially
words.example: The herald proclaimed the start of the tournament.

words.definition: to poke; to urge someone to act
words.example: She prodded the fire back into life with a stick.

words.definition: to make something last longer
words.example: They prolonged the holiday by an extra day.

words.definition: done without delay; punctual
words.example: Her prompt reply meant the tickets were saved.

words.definition: to express strong objection
words.example: The players protested about the muddy pitch.

words.definition: supplies of food and drink for a journey
words.example: They packed provisions for a week in the hills.

words.definition: to move around quietly, hunting or searching
words.example: A fox prowled along the hedge at dusk.

words.definition: to enquire too curiously into someone's private affairs
words.example: It's rude to pry into other people's letters.

words.definition: an angry argument
words.example: Their quarrel over the game was forgotten by teatime.

words.definition: a long search for something important
words.example: The knight set off on a quest for the lost crown.

words.definition: to search a place roughly, causing mess or damage
words.example: He ransacked his room looking for the permission slip.

words.definition: happening very quickly
words.example: The patient made a rapid recovery.

words.definition: a mischievous person, often said affectionately
words.example: The little rascal had hidden everyone's shoes.

words.definition: a fixed amount of food or supplies allowed to each person
words.example: Each climber carried a daily ration of chocolate.

words.definition: a deep, narrow valley with steep sides
words.example: A rope bridge swayed across the ravine.

words.definition: to move back or become more distant
words.example: The floodwater slowly receded from the fields.

words.definition: to say aloud from memory
words.example: Each pupil recited a poem at the assembly.

words.definition: to calculate; to be of the opinion
words.example: I reckon the journey will take two hours.

words.definition: to spring back suddenly in fear or disgust
words.example: She recoiled from the spider on the door handle.

words.definition: to tell someone about an event in detail
words.example: Grandpa recounted his adventures at sea.

words.definition: to enlist new members or workers
words.example: The choir recruited six new singers this term.

words.definition: to recover health and strength after illness
words.example: She recuperated at the seaside after the operation.

words.definition: to smell very strongly and unpleasantly
words.example: The changing room reeked of muddy boots.

words.definition: fit for a king or queen; magnificent
words.example: The swan glided past with regal calm.

words.definition: a feeling of sadness about something done or lost
words.example: His only regret was not saying goodbye properly.

words.definition: to rule as king or queen
words.example: The queen reigned for more than sixty years.

words.definition: to feel or show great joy
words.example: The village rejoiced when the team won the county cup.

words.definition: to enjoy greatly
words.example: She relished the first swim of the summer.

words.definition: a spoken or written comment
words.example: His kind remark about her painting made her day.

words.definition: a cure or solution for a problem
words.example: Honey and lemon is Gran's remedy for a sore throat.

words.definition: a small remaining part of something
words.example: Only a remnant of the castle wall still stands.

words.definition: an exact copy of something
words.example: The museum shop sells replicas of the golden mask.

words.definition: to feel bitter about something unfair
words.example: He resented being blamed for his brother's mess.

words.definition: to keep something for future use; to book
words.example: They reserved the best seats for the grandparents.

words.definition: to firmly decide; to settle a problem
words.example: She resolved to practise every morning before school.

words.definition: to bring back to its original condition
words.example: Volunteers restored the old carousel to working order.

words.definition: to move back or withdraw
words.example: The hikers retreated when the mist thickened.

words.definition: to get something back; to fetch
words.example: The spaniel retrieved the ball from the reeds.

words.definition: to bring back to life, consciousness, or strength
words.example: A drink of water revived the wilting seedlings.

words.definition: a violent disturbance by a crowd
words.example: Extra police were sent to prevent a riot.

words.definition: a small wave or series of waves
words.example: A single ripple spread across the silent lake.

words.definition: a person competing for the same thing
words.example: The two schools are old rivals on the rugby field.

words.definition: to wander over a wide area
words.example: Ponies roam freely across the moor.

words.definition: to search by moving things around carelessly
words.example: She rummaged in the drawer for a spare battery.

words.definition: typical of the countryside; simple and charming
words.example: They ate at a rustic table made from old barn doors.

words.definition: to make a soft crackling sound like dry leaves
words.example: Something rustled in the hedge beside the path.

words.definition: to rescue something from loss or destruction
words.example: They salvaged the photo albums from the flooded cellar.

words.definition: fierce and violent; wild
words.example: A savage gale tore tiles from the roofs.

words.definition: to run with quick, light steps
words.example: The squirrels scampered up the oak at our approach.

words.definition: to burn with very hot liquid or steam
words.example: Mind the kettle — the steam can scald you.

words.definition: to look at quickly to find something; to examine closely
words.example: She scanned the crowd for her father's red scarf.

words.definition: only just; almost not
words.example: He had scarcely sat down when the phone rang.

words.definition: to frown angrily
words.example: He scowled at the rain ruining his plans.

words.definition: to climb quickly using hands and feet; to rush
words.example: They scrambled over the rocks to the lighthouse.

words.definition: to write carelessly and untidily
words.example: He scrawled a note and taped it to the fridge.

words.definition: to move hurriedly with short quick steps
words.example: Mice scurried along the skirting board at night.

words.definition: to flow slowly through small openings
words.example: Rainwater seeped through the tent seams.

words.definition: to grab suddenly and firmly; to take control of
words.example: She seized the rope just before it slipped away.

words.definition: not often; rarely
words.example: It seldom snows here before Christmas.

words.definition: worn out and in poor condition; unfair
words.example: His shabby old coat had seen twenty winters.

words.definition: to break suddenly into many pieces
words.example: The ball shattered the greenhouse pane.

words.definition: to shine with a soft, flickering light
words.example: Heat made the road shimmer in the distance.

words.definition: having sharp judgement; clever in practical matters
words.example: A shrewd swap at the card fair doubled his collection.

words.definition: to tremble suddenly from fear, cold, or disgust
words.example: She shuddered at the thought of the spider cupboard.

words.definition: formal and dignified; deeply serious
words.example: The remembrance service ended with a solemn minute of silence.
